title: Transcript:25th Anniversary Livestream

{{No official name}}
{{Postbag|issue=39|date=27 February 2026|url=https://youtu.be/D4WYQo96z_0?t=6947|osrs=yes}}
{{Transcript list|Romeo|Romily Weaklax|Cow31337Killer|Drunken Dwarf|Bank of Gielinor|Chaos Elemental|noplayer}}

''This Postbag from the Hedge was read out loud on livestream ([https://youtu.be/D4WYQo96z_0?t=6947 recording here]), and manually transcribed. It may not be 100% accurate.''

==To Romeo==
{{Letter|Dear Romeo, I hope you are doing well. I would like to have an update on your exciting love life.

Yours truly, 

Chill N Kill}}

I'm sorry, but I don't think I know any Kill N Chill. I'm guessing you must be one of my admirers. Well, I'm afraid to say that I am now taken. Phillipa and I are very much in love. She's an absolute fox, and I'm saving up to buy her the finest third-hand jewellery in all of Varrock.

In fact, I was recently asked to help set up a venue for a thrilling upcoming musical performance. The money I get from that will buy all the jewellery Phillipa could ever want. We were hoping to use the party room in Falador, but apparently it was already booked by the Acheron Apes. 

Anyway, I don't think it's going to be a problem. The good man who I intend to buy the third-hand jewellery from has offered to let me use his POH. His name is... I think, Mug Ger. It's an odd name, but he seems an all around lovely fellow, combat level 6 I believe. 

Anyways, I hope you find your own love soon, Kill N Chill. Although... Phillipa has said something about one of her family dying abruptly, I was just thinking if anything happens to Phillipa herself, I'll um, certainly write back to you.

Yours sincerely, 

Romeo

==To Romily Weaklax==
{{Letter|Hello fellow pie lover, I have recently taken a shipment of your wonderful pies up to Rellekka for some of your friends and I was just wondering something: How do you get your pies to last such a long journey without spoiling?

The harsh sea air and all manner of elements surely would turn a lesser pie stale and soggy. But by the time we landed, they seemed as pristine as when I loaded them. The fine gentleman who collected the pies from me seemed to be sure they'd be even fine on the second trip even further north.

You must tell me your secrets so I can protect all the summer pies I'll be making in the near future.

Your friend in pie-hood, 

Burnt Imp}}

Oh, Burnt Imp, it's so good to hear from a fellow pie connoisseur. I'm glad to hear that you've noticed how good my pies are. As you say, any lesser pie would go stale long before they ever make it up that far north. 

Indeed, I do believe I am the only pie salesman capable of having pies delivered that far. As for the secret, well, I'm afraid it's a closely guarded one, passed down over generations of the Weaklax family. 

Truth be told, I can't even be sure if the origin's a secret anymore. However, it is said that the great Durant Weaklax discovered the secret back when our family still lived in colder climes up north. 

Anyway, while I'm afraid I can't share my secrets, I still very much wish you all the best in your pie making endeavors.

All the best, 

Romily Weaklax

==To Cow31337Killer==
{{Letter|Hi mate, hope you're doing well. I haven't seen you since I was helping Ava out. Heard there was a new cow boss coming to Lumbridge.

Have you got any good ideas on how you can take down this boss? I remember you distinctly had a thing for killing cows. So how bad is the cow boss hard mode?

Cheers,

Timmy McFly}}

Hullo Timmy McFly. The news of a cow boss was a surprise to me. I thought they were too disorganized for a leadership structure. Still, it remains my mission to be a beacon of security in this cow-infested world. So I intend to tackle this problem head-on. If I had to guess, it would still be a cow at heart, which means with my elite talents, it shall fall quite comfortably to my axe. 

Catch you on the flipside. 

Cow31337Killer

==To the Drunken Dwarf==
{{Letter|Hello Mr. Drunken Dwarf, my favorite chum. I never tire of your cheeky pint and early dinner, but I've been wondering, where do you source it all from? Me and the lads have been making a bit of a game you see, and we want to settle this once and for all.

Seal Neko
}}

Ey, Seal Neko, me good matey! 

It's all went and started when I went off on Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl, it was a great day out for me and me mateys. Anyway, while we were having a cheeky Fire Brandy over at the Dragon Inn in Yanille, I went and got myself a bit lost! I accidentally wandered into some house of some magic man, or something, and he had this door, um, but it wasn't a door, it wasn't like a door, it wasn't a window either, it was—magic! Something magic, yes, so I put me hand in it cause you do, right, right, and what did I pull out? A kebab! 

So the weird thing is though, ever since then, I just keep finding kebabs everywhere. It's a lot of good fun but it's also a bit of a nightmare—only way to get rid of them is to keep finding more mateys to share them with! As for the beer, I just buy that over at The Laughing Miner.

All the best matey.

==To the Bank of Gielinor==
{{Letter|Hello, I'm concerned for the well-being of bank employees throughout Gielinor. Are the bankers paid living wages? Do the bankers in remote or dangerous areas, like Gundai, in the wildy Mage Arena bank, get hazard pay? And why haven't the Port Phasmatys bankers chosen to move on? Surely they cannot be so keen to wile away their ghostly days from behind a bank booth.

Zulrah Mamdani
}}

Good day Zulrah Mamdani. Your concern for the welfare of our employees is admirable. We would like to reaffirm that yes, all employees working for the Bank of Gielinor are paid at a fair and appropriate rate for the work they do. 

We are unfortunately unable to reveal any details of individual arrangements for our employees, however we can confirm that they have access to any and all appropriate protection that they may request, especially for those operating in high risk areas. 

As for our "vitally challenged" employees, it is entirely their own choice as to whether if they decide to move on as you suggest, and we are not in a position to encourage them in any direction. If they are unhappy, they are welcome to raise their concerns. 

Kind regards and happy banking. 

Management for the Bank of Gielinor

==To the Chaos Elemental== 
{{Letter|Dear Chaos Elemental, what would you do if your name was Grumby Stumbaloo?}}
<!--The response to this letter is verbatim provided by Mod Ash-->
GMars,

Verily, I didst make sojourn unto the local Graveyard in the yestereve, yet did I espy a knave upon the strand, for whom a bath was sorely the necessity. The waters were as gelid as snow, yet an eternal brazier wouldst set aright the perturbance of mine host. Alas, he didst not possess one thereof, yet mayhaps his child didst.

In any matter, doth it not pose a great endeavour to lose thine own loved ones? Indeed, I didst leave one in a casket for many a year, though be not in fear, for she didst appear unharmed. Yet, 'tis a thing that stirs the mind, doth it not? For in sooth, we are such stuff as dreams are made on.

But lo, I do find myself entwined in mine own cogitations. For, let us not forget, the cause of our assembly hither. 'Tis, if it pleases thee, the moon. All seem to be besotted with wonder of it in the present, but, haply, they do forget the aspect of most import. But, be not dismayed, for I deem they shall recall in season soon.

Ah, but hearken well, fair companion, for the time of departure draweth nigh. I shall impart unto thee certain words of wit, ere I take mine leave. Have naught to do with yonder alt oaks, but rather beware of what lieth hid within their ancient hearts.

Yours,

Stumby Grumbaloo

{{Postbag from the Hedge}}
